nodes.label: lib package
nodes.description: The Python package backing brAIn at lib/. Contains all semantic and persistence logic: db (Kuzu setup), slugify (id canonicalization), validate (payload validation), ingest (4-phase upsert pipeline), query (read-side traversals), audit (graph health), merge (explicit node merge), export_import (full dump/restore), logs (JSONL append-only audit logs). Imported by brain.py, mcp_server.py, ui/backend/main.py, brain_stop_check.py and experiments/ scripts. Zero LLM calls — the intelligence lives in Claude, lib is pure plumbing.

nodes.label: lib db module
nodes.description: lib/db.py — Kuzu connection helpers plus the canonical DDL for the Node and Rel tables. Exposes connect(db_path) (creates the directory if missing, returns a kuzu.Connection), init_schema(conn) (idempotent CREATE NODE/REL TABLE IF NOT EXISTS), and the result-materialization helpers rows()/one() that turn Kuzu QueryResults into list-of-dict / single-dict for the rest of the code.

nodes.label: DEFAULT_DB_PATH constant
nodes.description: Resolved once from PROJECT_ROOT = lib/db.py's parent.parent / 'graph' / 'kuzu_db'. Single source of truth for where the embedded Kuzu DB lives. Every consumer (CLI, MCP, UI backend, hooks, experiments) imports this rather than hardcoding a path — supports the 'installable anywhere' guarantee.

nodes.label: Idempotent schema setup
nodes.description: init_schema(conn) runs CREATE NODE/REL TABLE IF NOT EXISTS, so it is safe to call on every connection. Every entry point (`brain.py init`, `brain.py ingest`, mcp_server.brain_ingest, etc.) calls init_schema after connecting, so missing-schema errors cannot occur at runtime — the first command that touches the DB sets it up.

nodes.label: rows and one helpers
nodes.description: Materialization helpers in lib/db.py. Kuzu's QueryResult is iterator-style with positional rows; rows() zips column names with values to produce list[dict], one() returns the first row or None. Centralizes the dict-conversion so query/audit/ingest/merge don't repeat the boilerplate, and so positional changes in a Cypher RETURN clause only need one fix.

nodes.label: lib slugify module
nodes.description: lib/slugify.py — the only place id canonicalization is implemented. Sequence: NFKD-decompose to strip accents, lowercase, replace any run of non-alphanumerics by '_', collapse repeats, strip leading/trailing '_', truncate to MAX_LEN=80 preferring the last '_' boundary in the second half. Raises SlugifyError if the result is empty (label was entirely non-ASCII punctuation).

nodes.label: MAX_LEN 80 truncation
nodes.description: Ids are truncated to 80 characters. When truncation is needed, the slugifier prefers to cut on the last '_' boundary in the second half of the result so the id ends on a whole word rather than a half-word. Caps the worst-case id length without producing readable but non-canonical fragments.

nodes.label: SlugifyError class
nodes.description: ValueError subclass raised when a label cannot be turned into a non-empty slug (label is None, or pure non-ASCII punctuation). Caught in validate._validate_node and converted into a 'label_unslugifiable' rejection so the rest of the payload can still ingest.

nodes.label: lib validate module
nodes.description: lib/validate.py — payload validation BEFORE any DB write. Splits the incoming JSON into a ValidationResult: nodes/rels ready for insertion, rejected_nodes (missing label/type, unslugifiable, not-a-dict), rejected_rels (type-not-whitelisted, missing endpoints, self-loop, not-a-dict), rewritten_ids (proposed id ≠ slugify(label)), extension_node_types (out-of-reference but accepted). Encodes the open-node / strict-rel policy in one place.

nodes.label: REFERENCE_NODE_TYPES constant
nodes.description: Frozen set of the 11 reference node types in lib/validate.py: concept, entity, event, claim, mechanism, algorithm, property, person, place, artifact, process. Used for SOFT comparison — nodes whose type is outside the set are still ingested but logged to extension_requests.jsonl. Drives the open-vocabulary policy on the node side.

nodes.label: REL_TYPES whitelist constant
nodes.description: Union of CAUSAL_REL_TYPES (causes, prevents, requires, enables, precedes, contradicts), STRUCTURAL_REL_TYPES (is_a, part_of, instance_of, similar_to, property_of), and FALLBACK_REL_TYPES (related_to). HARD constraint: any relation whose type is not in this set is rejected at validation time. The split into three subsets is for readability — they all live in one membership check.

nodes.label: NodePayload dataclass
nodes.description: Normalized node ready for insertion: id (canonical), label (stripped), type (string), description (stripped, default empty), importance (clamped to [0,1], default 0.5). The dataclass shape is what the upsert phase consumes — validate produces it, ingest consumes it, no other code parses raw dicts.

nodes.label: RelPayload dataclass
nodes.description: Normalized relation ready for insertion: src, dst, type, confidence (clamped to [0,1], default 0.8), evidence (stripped, default empty), factor (stripped, default empty). 'factor' is an optional one-word qualifier shown next to the evidence in CLI/UI output (e.g. mathematical factor name).

nodes.label: Importance and confidence clamping
nodes.description: Both importance (nodes) and confidence (rels) are clamped to [0.0, 1.0] during validation; if the raw value is not numeric, the default is restored (0.5 for importance, 0.8 for confidence). Prevents pathological payloads (importance=-1, confidence='high') from breaking downstream aggregation and ordering.

nodes.label: ValidationResult dataclass
nodes.description: Bundle produced by validate_payload. Carries the accepted normalized payloads (nodes, rels) AND four parallel lists of validation artifacts: rejected_nodes, rejected_rels, rewritten_ids, extension_node_types. The ingest pipeline then logs the artifacts before persisting the accepted lists, keeping the validation/persistence concerns separate.

nodes.label: lib ingest module
nodes.description: lib/ingest.py — the 4-phase upsert pipeline: (1) validate_payload returns canonical nodes/rels + rejection artifacts; (2) _record_validation_artifacts writes the artifacts to extension_requests.jsonl; (3) _purge_doc_contributions removes the doc_id from every Rel.sources/Node.sources it appears in (deleting empty-sources rels, never deleting nodes); (4) _upsert_nodes then _upsert_rels insert or merge. Produces an IngestReport with per-phase counters consumed by CLI/MCP/UI.

nodes.label: IngestReport dataclass
nodes.description: Return value of ingest_payload. Carries counters (nodes_created, nodes_updated, rels_created, rels_updated, rels_purged, rels_purged_deleted) and lists (rejected_nodes, rejected_rels, rewritten_ids, potential_duplicates, skipped_rels). brain.py prints a one-line summary of each field, the UI displays it back to the human, and experiments accumulate totals across documents.

nodes.label: Purge phase
nodes.description: First write phase of re-ingest. Finds every Rel whose sources contains the incoming doc_id, extracts the index of that doc_id and removes the parallel evidences/factors entries; if the resulting sources is empty the rel is DELETED (counted as rels_purged_deleted), otherwise the trimmed arrays are written back (rels_purged). Nodes are NEVER deleted — their sources list is just trimmed. Makes re-ingestion atomic w.r.t. the doc_id without disturbing other documents' contributions.

nodes.label: Upsert nodes phase
nodes.description: Second write phase. For each validated node: if a node with that id already exists, merge — refresh label, fill description ONLY if currently empty, importance = max(old, new), sources = dedup union with new doc_id. If sources was empty (sole-owner re-ingest case) treat as fresh: accept new description and importance. Else create a brand-new node and call _log_substring_duplicates to flag near-duplicates.

nodes.label: Sole-owner re-ingest branch
nodes.description: Subtle ingest behavior: during the purge phase a node's sources may become empty (the doc just purged was its only owner). In that case the subsequent upsert treats it as a fresh node — accepts the new description and importance instead of preserving the old. Without this branch, editing a document that originally introduced a node would keep stale metadata forever.

nodes.label: First non-empty description wins rule
nodes.description: On normal multi-doc updates the node's description is preserved if non-empty; the new one is only used to fill an empty description. Rationale: the first extraction to actually capture a comprehensive description should not be silently downgraded by a later passing mention. To rewrite a description, the user must edit the original doc and re-ingest (which triggers the sole-owner branch if it was the only source).

nodes.label: Substring duplicate lookup
nodes.description: _log_substring_duplicates runs only on NEW node creation. Cypher: WHERE lower(x.label) CONTAINS $needle OR $needle CONTAINS lower(x.label), LIMIT 5. Self matches filtered out by id. If any candidate is found, the record is logged to potential_duplicates.jsonl and added to IngestReport.potential_duplicates — the pipeline NEVER auto-merges. User reviews the log and runs `brain.py merge SRC INTO DST` explicitly.

nodes.label: DUPLICATE_LOOKUP_LIMIT 5
nodes.description: Constant in lib/ingest.py capping the substring-duplicate lookup at 5 candidates per new node. Keeps the log readable and the Cypher fast even when the graph holds tens of thousands of nodes with shared prefixes (e.g. neural_network, neural_network_resurgence_1980s).

nodes.label: Upsert rels phase
nodes.description: Third write phase. For each rel, resolves endpoints: if r.src exists as a node id, use it; else slugify(r.src) (allows payloads that reference labels instead of ids). If after this either endpoint is missing, the rel is RECORDED in IngestReport.skipped_rels (reason 'missing_endpoint') and skipped. Else look up existing edge with same (src, dst, type): if present, max-confidence and append to evidences/factors/sources arrays; else CREATE a new edge.

nodes.label: Missing endpoint silent skip
nodes.description: Rel resolution is forgiving: it slugifies the proposed src/dst before declaring it missing, so a payload that mixes label-strings and ids still mostly works. But if the slugified form also fails, the rel is skipped (NOT failed) and reported in skipped_rels. The user is expected to read the ingest report — silent skipping in production graphs is the most common cause of disconnected nodes. The README warning about parentheses/slashes in labels exists because of this.

nodes.label: Parallel arrays invariant
nodes.description: Each Rel's sources[], evidences[], factors[] arrays must stay the SAME length, with positionally-aligned entries (evidences[i] and factors[i] come from sources[i]). Maintained by the upsert and purge phases. Violation is fatal for traceability — audit explicitly checks size(sources)==size(evidences) and raises an integrity error if not.

nodes.label: lib query module
nodes.description: lib/query.py — pure read-side traversals. find_nodes (substring + ordering by importance), show_node (node plus outgoing/incoming with evidence), walk (generic BFS by rel-type subset and direction), causes_of (BFS upstream via CAUSAL_BACKWARD), effects_of (BFS downstream via CAUSAL_FORWARD), paths (variable-length Cypher up to max_hops), stats (counts by type), context_for_topic (substring pivots + 1-hop neighborhood, used as agentic preflight), run_cypher (raw escape hatch).

nodes.label: CAUSAL_FORWARD subset
nodes.description: Subset of relation types walked when traversing 'effects': causes, enables, precedes. INTENTIONALLY excludes 'requires' — A `requires` B means B must exist before A, so from A's perspective requires goes UPSTREAM not DOWNSTREAM. Mixing it in would produce incorrect effect chains. Excludes contradicts/prevents because they are blockers, not causal arrows.

nodes.label: CAUSAL_BACKWARD subset
nodes.description: Subset of relation types walked when traversing 'causes' upstream: causes, requires, enables, precedes. Includes 'requires' (asymmetric vs CAUSAL_FORWARD): if A requires B then B is something A needs to exist, i.e. a precondition / cause from A's perspective.

nodes.label: BFS level-by-level walk
nodes.description: lib.query.walk performs breadth-first traversal one frontier at a time, deduplicating visited node ids. Returns a list of levels — each level is a list of row-dicts produced by a single Cypher batch for the current frontier. Letting the caller see distinct levels (vs one flat list) is what makes `brain causes X` print `-- level 1 --`, `-- level 2 --` etc., which is much easier for humans to reason about than a flat chain.

nodes.label: Paths variable-length query
nodes.description: paths() uses Kuzu's variable-length pattern: MATCH p = (a)-[:Rel*1..max_hops]->(b) RETURN nodes(p), rels(p). Default max_hops=4, limit=10 — keeps the query bounded on dense graphs. Returns nodes and rels of each path separately so the CLI can render arrow chains. NOT restricted to causal subsets — the user may want any path (e.g. property_of + is_a) to explain connectivity.

nodes.label: context_for_topic function
nodes.description: Returns the substring matches for a topic, each enriched with the top-K outgoing and incoming edges (ordered by confidence). Search is case-insensitive over id/label/description. Designed to be JSON-piped into an LLM prompt as 'here is what the graph already knows about this topic, reuse existing ids' — the heuristic-preflight precursor to the agentic query_graph tool. Used by `brain context`, the MCP brain_show context, and the UI /api/node endpoint.

nodes.label: find_nodes substring search
nodes.description: Case-insensitive substring match over id and label, ordered by importance DESC then label ASC, default limit 30. Intentionally narrow (no fuzzy match, no embedding lookup) — substring + importance ordering is the cheapest signal that still catches synonyms and pluralizations 90% of the time. Phase 4 will add embeddings.

nodes.label: lib audit module
nodes.description: lib/audit.py — read-only graph health report. Computes volumes (counts by type), related_to ratio, no-description ratio, orphan ratio (and sample), single-source rel count, top-degree nodes (in/out), avg confidence per rel type, contributions by document, and array-alignment integrity check. Returns AuditReport(metrics, warnings, errors) with an exit_code property: 0 OK, 1 warnings, 2 errors. Drives `brain audit` CI-style health checks.

nodes.label: Audit thresholds
nodes.description: Three module-level constants in lib/audit.py: RELATED_TO_THRESHOLD = 0.05 (5% of edges as related_to → warning), NO_DESCRIPTION_THRESHOLD = 0.20 (20% of nodes without description → warning), ORPHAN_THRESHOLD = 0.10 (10% of nodes without any edge → warning). Crossing a threshold appends a warning; the exit_code surface lets CI treat warnings vs errors differently.

nodes.label: Array alignment integrity check
nodes.description: audit._array_alignment runs MATCH ()-[r:Rel]->() WHERE size(r.sources) <> size(r.evidences) RETURN count(*). Any non-zero count is an ERROR (not just a warning) — it means the parallel-arrays invariant is broken and traceability is corrupted. The exit_code becomes 2 and CI fails immediately.

nodes.label: Orphan node detection
nodes.description: Audit query: MATCH (n:Node) WHERE NOT EXISTS { MATCH (n)-[:Rel]-() }. Returns the count + 20-sample. Orphans are nodes the extractor created without linking — typically because the extraction skipped the relation pass or the rel was silently dropped via the missing-endpoint pitfall. Warning fires above 10% orphan ratio.

nodes.label: Doc contributions metric
nodes.description: audit._doc_contributions unwinds the sources arrays on both nodes and rels and groups by doc_id, producing nodes/rels counts per source document. Lets the user spot a document that contributed only nodes (no rels) — usually means the extractor stopped before the relation pass.

nodes.label: Audit exit_code property
nodes.description: AuditReport.exit_code: 0 if no errors and no warnings, 1 if warnings only, 2 if any error. Wired through `brain audit` so the command can be used as a CI health gate — non-zero exit fails the build. Distinction between warnings and errors is intentional: ratio thresholds are heuristics (warn) while array misalignment is a hard contract (error).

nodes.label: lib merge module
nodes.description: lib/merge.py — explicit `brain.py merge SRC INTO DST` implementation. Reads both nodes (errors if either missing), redirects every SRC→X outgoing edge to DST→X (dropping the self-loop SRC→DST if it existed), redirects every X→SRC incoming edge to X→DST, merges metadata (max importance, dst.description preferred), DETACH DELETEs SRC. Returns counts (outgoing_moved, incoming_moved) for the CLI summary.

nodes.label: Merge collision absorption
nodes.description: _absorb_or_create_rel: when redirecting an edge to DST, if DST already has an edge of the same type to/from the same neighbor, the incoming evidences/sources are CONCATENATED (not deduped) and confidence is set to max. Equivalent to running the upsert pipeline manually but in the merge context. Means a merge after two parallel ingests does not lose any evidence — it just unifies the two arrays.

nodes.label: Self-loop drop on merge
nodes.description: If SRC has an outgoing edge to DST (or symmetric incoming), redirecting it would produce DST→DST. Merge silently DROPS such edges to keep the self-loop ban intact. Caller is warned only via the moved-count being lower than expected, not via an explicit message.

nodes.label: Merge irreversibility
nodes.description: `brain.py merge` is irreversible — SRC is DETACH DELETEd, its original description is lost unless DST's description was empty (in which case SRC's is inherited). User responsibility: review potential_duplicates.jsonl, decide which side is canonical, then merge. The pipeline never auto-merges because cross-document equivalence is a semantic judgement, not a substring match.

nodes.label: lib export_import module
nodes.description: lib/export_import.py — full graph dump/restore. export_graph(conn) → dict with version, exported_at, nodes (all properties), rels (src/dst plus all rel properties). write_export writes that dict to JSON. import_graph(payload, strategy=force|merge) loads it back: force wipes the DB first (MATCH ()-[r]->() DELETE r; MATCH (n) DETACH DELETE n), merge keeps existing data and cumulates sources/evidences on collisions.

nodes.label: Export version field
nodes.description: EXPORT_VERSION = '0.1' stamped into every export. Currently informational; reserved so future schema changes can drive a migration step at import time instead of failing silently. Format is single JSON document with version, exported_at (UTC ISO), nodes[], rels[].

nodes.label: Import force strategy
nodes.description: Default of `brain.py import`. Wipes all rels and nodes first, then inserts cleanly. Use to RESTORE a known-good state from a dump. Refuses unknown strategy strings up-front (ValueError) — only 'force' and 'merge' are accepted.

nodes.label: Import merge strategy
nodes.description: Alternative strategy of `brain.py import`. Keeps existing data and CUMULATES on collisions: for each node already present, sources = dedup union, importance = max, description filled only if empty. For each rel already present (same triple), confidence = max, sources and evidences are concatenated (parallel arrays). Use to MERGE a dump from another machine into the current graph.

nodes.label: lib logs module
nodes.description: lib/logs.py — JSONL append-only audit logs. log_jsonl writes one JSON line (with UTC ts prefix) to a given filename under the project root. log_extension_request emits node/rel-type extension events. log_potential_duplicate emits substring-overlap candidates. The two filename constants (EXTENSION_REQUESTS, POTENTIAL_DUPLICATES) are the contract — they are also listed in .gitignore.

nodes.label: JSONL append-only format
nodes.description: Both audit logs are JSONL: one JSON object per line, appended never rewritten. Lets grep/jq/awk inspect them without parsing the whole file, and lets concurrent writers (e.g. parallel experiment runs) tail-append safely. Files are git-ignored — treated as transient operational telemetry, not project artifacts.

nodes.label: factors array field
nodes.description: Optional STRING[] on the Rel table, parallel to sources[]/evidences[]. Stores a one-word mathematical or qualitative factor (e.g. 'k', 'reliability_sigmoid') that callers can use to label the cause/effect intensity without bloating the evidence sentence. Surfaced in CLI 'f=' annotation and in the UI rel-factor field. Empty strings are filtered out at display time.

rows() and one() helpers exposed by lib/db.py.

Defined in lib/validate.py; produced by validate, consumed by ingest.

_purge_doc_contributions is phase 1 of the ingest pipeline.
_upsert_nodes is phase 2 of the ingest pipeline.
_upsert_rels is phase 3 of the ingest pipeline.
Purge must complete before upsert so the sole-owner branch can trigger correctly.
Nodes must exist before rels can resolve their endpoints.
Purging the doc_id contributions before re-inserting is what makes re-ingest idempotent.
Implemented as a conditional inside _upsert_nodes.
Codified in _upsert_nodes: description filled only if previously empty (except in the sole-owner branch).
_log_substring_duplicates called on every NEW node creation in _upsert_nodes.
Every hit produces one line in potential_duplicates.jsonl.

Implemented as a check inside _upsert_rels.
When a payload references the pre-rewrite id, the upsert tries r.src, then slugify(r.src), and if neither exists silently records the skip.
Purge must remove the same index from sources/evidences/factors to preserve alignment.
Upsert must append to all three arrays together to preserve alignment.
Without alignment, evidences[i] and sources[i] are no longer linked, breaking traceability.
factors STRING[] is one of the Rel-table columns.
factors[] must stay aligned with sources[] and evidences[] under purge and upsert.
Constant CAUSAL_FORWARD defined in lib/query.py.
Constant CAUSAL_BACKWARD defined in lib/query.py.
The two subsets disagree on `requires` — it is upstream-only, not downstream — so they cannot be unified into a single 'causal' subset.
Generic walk() function in lib/query.py used by causes_of and effects_of.
paths() in lib/query.py uses Kuzu's variable-length pattern.
Implemented in lib/query.py; used by `brain context`, MCP, and UI.
Provides the substring+neighborhood data the agentic query_graph tool returns to Claude.

Module-level constants in lib/audit.py.

Detects violations of the parallel-arrays invariant after the fact, providing the safety net.

The 5% related_to threshold makes the 'minimize related_to' rule operational.

Implemented as guards in _redirect_outgoing and _redirect_incoming.
Merge preserves the global self-loop ban by silently dropping any redirected edge that would become DST→DST.
SRC is DETACH DELETEd at the end of merge_nodes; there is no undo.
The intended workflow is: review potential_duplicates.jsonl, then call merge explicitly — the log feeds the merge.
EXPORT_VERSION constant in lib/export_import.py.
force is one of two strategies in import_graph.
merge is one of two strategies in import_graph.
Force wipes existing data; merge keeps it — strictly mutually exclusive strategies for the same import operation.
The append-only contract is implemented in lib/logs.log_jsonl.
log_extension_request writes to extension_requests.jsonl.
log_potential_duplicate writes to potential_duplicates.jsonl.
